generated_parts.label: Integration Harness + Datum Spine
generated_parts.spec: Integration_harness closure spine with harness_channel, connector_keying, assembly_datum pads, routing clips, service hatch, reciprocal interface labels, inspection checklist marks

verify.value: Battery, sensor, actuator modules remain removable
verify.rule: [replaceable_module_path] good: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown. | bad: Functional modules are fused into a closed body.

[cable_harness_visibility] good: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels. | bad: Cables are buried inside closed links or shells with no inspection feature.
[access_not_just_text] good: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover. | bad: Access is claimed only in prose.

verify.value: Strain relief and bend radius respected at connector exits
verify.rule: [harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius] good: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges. | bad: Cables exit bare holes, cross hinge lines unsupported, or bend around sharp printed edges with no relief feature.

[connector_accessibility_and_keying] good: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating. | bad: Connectors face closed walls, sit in unreachable pockets, or identical adjacent connectors have no keying or label.

verify.value: Datum pads are visible and labeled
verify.rule: [datum_visibility] good: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads are visible and labeled. | bad: Interfaces exist only as abstract neighbor references.
[clearance_named] good: Critical slip, snap, pin, hinge, and slide interfaces state plausible clearances. | bad: Moving/removable joints have no clearance or fit language.
[interface_count] good: Each subsystem has at least three adjacent interfaces or datums. | bad: Subsystem is isolated and does not show assembly neighbors.

[standard_fit_vocabulary] good: Fits are named with standard intent: clearance fit for sliding, transition for locating, interference/press only where justified, with a numeric allowance. | bad: All mating diameters are modeled line-to-line with identical nominal dimensions and no fit class or allowance.

[standard_hardware_dimensioning] good: Holes, slots, and pockets match real catalog hardware sizes (M3/M4 clearance holes, standard bearing OD, common pin diameters). | bad: Hardware features use arbitrary dimensions that match no purchasable fastener, bearing, or pin.
[wall_process_readiness] good: Material/process, wall assumptions, build direction, and supports are explicit enough for design review. | bad: Manufacturing process is absent or contradicts geometry.
cad_brief.mass_est_g must be a numeric grams value
[inspection_traceability] good: Verify/risk items reference real features or interfaces from part_tree/geometry_ops. | bad: Verification is generic pass/warn text not tied to features.

risk.desc: Connector mismating due to lack of keying
risk.mit: Implement connector keying and labeling
Ensure adequate strain relief at cable exits

physics_paths: Load path: Battery tray -> Spine -> Payload mounting rails
Data path: Connector keying -> Harness channel -> Avionics bay connector
Thermal path: Conductive vias in spine -> Heatsink interface
integration_opportunities: Co-location of harness routing and connector keying for efficient assembly
Integration of datum pads with mounting interfaces for improved stability

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum pad on Battery Tray interface

verification_focus: Harness strain relief and bend radius at connector exits
